name: Supabase MCP
what_it_does: Supabase's official MCP server provides AI agents with direct access to manage Supabase projects including PostgreSQL databases, authentication, storage, and edge functions.
use_cases: Running SQL queries against Supabase PostgreSQL databases from agents
Managing database schema changes including table and column operations
Interacting with Supabase Auth for user management automation
Querying and managing Supabase Storage buckets and files
Creating and deploying Supabase Edge Functions via agents
not_for: Non-Supabase PostgreSQL databases (use direct pg drivers instead)
Projects needing proprietary database features (use RDS/Cloud SQL)
best_when: An agent needs to interact with a Supabase-hosted PostgreSQL project, especially for schema management, data querying, or managing Supabase-specific features like Auth and Storage.
avoid_when: You are not using Supabase, or you need to manage a non-Supabase PostgreSQL database.

auth.notes: Supabase personal access token (PAT) for MCP server auth. Project-specific anon and service_role keys for client SDK. Service role key grants full database access — handle carefully.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 500MB database, 1GB storage, 50,000 MAU, 500K edge function invocations
pricing.notes: Very generous free tier. Pro plan needed for production workloads with higher limits. Paused projects after 1 week inactivity on free tier.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by plan; PostgREST rate limits configurable

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: PostgreSQL errors are surfaced directly with full error messages. PostgREST layer adds additional context. SQL errors include line and column information.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: PostgreSQL supports ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING/UPDATE for true upserts. Migrations tracked via schema_migrations table.
known_agent_gotchas: Free tier projects pause after 1 week of inactivity — first request after pause has cold start
Service role key bypasses Row Level Security — agents using it have full unfiltered access
MCP server requires npx to run — adds Node.js dependency
Database branch management (multiple environments) requires Pro plan
Storage file operations return URLs that expire — agents must handle URL refresh
Edge functions require Deno runtime knowledge — different from Node.js
